title: Research Library
description: Peer-reviewed research linking grip strength, functional health and multifactorial screening to falls, frailty, mortality and clinical outcomes. The evidence base behind Able Assess.

seo.title: Research Library | Able Care
seo.description: Peer-reviewed research linking grip strength and functional health to falls, frailty, mortality and clinical outcomes. Browse the evidence base behind Able Assess.

blocks.headline: Every claim backed by the evidence.
blocks.subtitle: Browse the peer-reviewed research behind Able Assess. Our own publications on the GripAble sensor, plus the wider literature on grip strength and falls prevention, all in one place.

blocks.heading: How this library is organized
blocks.body: The library is grouped into three sections. Our Publications are peer-reviewed studies on the GripAble sensor that underpins Able Assess — reliability, precision, inter-instrument agreement with Jamar and normative reference values. Papers on Grip Strength covers the wider literature establishing grip strength as a vital-sign-grade biomarker of ageing, cardiovascular disease, cancer, respiratory disease and all-cause mortality. Papers on Falls Prevention gathers the guideline-level evidence (World Guidelines 2022, NICE NG249 2025, Cochrane) alongside the functional-health and readmission studies that make the operational case for objective, multifactorial screening.

blocks.heading: Our Publications
blocks.pulledStat: GripAble has excellent inter-instrument reliability with Jamar, the incumbent gold standard.

citations.title: Remote, Reliable, Repeatable: Real-World Test, Retest Validation of Hand Grip Strength Assessments
citations.finding: Remote grip strength assessment with GripAble shows high reliability in real-world settings.

citations.title: GripAble has excellent test-retest reliability with Jamar
citations.finding: GripAble demonstrates excellent test-retest reliability compared to the Jamar dynamometer.

citations.title: Test-Retest Reliability and Precision of GripAble, A Multi-Purpose Exergaming Device
citations.finding: GripAble's reliability to assess maximal hand grip strength is confirmed in controlled contexts.

citations.title: Interrater Reliability and Precision of a Novel Hand Strength Assessment and Treatment Device: The GripAble
citations.journal: The American Journal of Occupational Therapy
citations.finding: GripAble offers accuracy and reliability suitable for occupational therapy practitioners.

citations.title: GripAble: Interrater Reliability and Normative Grip Strength of UK Population
citations.finding: Normative reference values established for UK adults using GripAble, enabling population-level comparison.

citations.title: Modernising grip dynamometry: Inter-instrument reliability between GripAble and Jamar
citations.finding: GripAble has good-to-excellent inter-instrument reliability compared to Jamar Plus+.

citations.title: GripAble: An Accurate, Sensitive and Robust Digital Device for Measuring Grip Strength
citations.journal: Journal of Rehabilitation and Assistive Technologies Engineering
citations.finding: GripAble is a highly robust, accurate, reliable grip strength dynamometer for clinical and research use.

blocks.heading: Papers on Grip Strength
blocks.pulledStat: Grip strength predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure.

citations.title: Prognostic value of grip strength: findings from the Prospective Urban Rural Epidemiology (PURE) study
citations.finding: Grip strength was a stronger predictor of all-cause and cardiovascular mortality than systolic blood pressure across 17 countries.

citations.title: Grip Strength: An Indispensable Biomarker For Older Adults
citations.finding: Grip strength is an indispensable biomarker for predicting outcomes in older adults across multiple health domains.

citations.title: Grip strength and mortality: a biomarker of ageing?
citations.finding: Grip strength serves as a biomarker of biological aging, predicting mortality across all age groups.

citations.title: Hand grip strength as a proposed new vital sign of health: a narrative review
citations.journal: Journal of Health and Population Nutrition
citations.finding: Grip strength proposed as a new vital sign of health, with evidence supporting its role across the lifespan.

citations.title: Handgrip strength and health outcomes: Umbrella review of systematic reviews with meta-analyzes
citations.journal: Journal of Sport and Health Science
citations.finding: Comprehensive meta-analysis confirming grip strength predicts cardiovascular disease, cancer, respiratory disease and all-cause mortality.

blocks.heading: Papers on Falls Prevention
blocks.pulledStat: Risk-stratified, multifactorial falls interventions reduce fall incidence by 25–35%.

citations.title: World Guidelines on Falls Prevention and Management in Older Adults (Montero-Odasso et al.)
citations.finding: International consensus guideline setting the 3-tier falls risk stratification approach and Grade 1A evidence for gait speed as a screening tool.

citations.title: NICE Guideline NG249: Falls in older people — assessment and prevention
citations.journal: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
citations.finding: UK clinical guideline. Single screening tools identify only 40–60% of at-risk community-dwelling older adults; multifactorial interventions based on comprehensive assessment reduce fall incidence by 32–40%.

citations.title: Interventions for preventing falls in older people living in the community
citations.finding: Meta-analysis of multifactorial and exercise-based interventions; risk-stratified programs reduce fall incidence by 25–35%.

citations.title: Predictive validity of the Timed Up and Go test for falls in older adults: a meta-analysis
citations.finding: Timed Up and Go time of ≥14 seconds predicts falls with 81% sensitivity across 21 studies.

citations.title: WHO Step Safely: Strategies for the prevention and management of falls across the life-course
citations.finding: WHO framework on multifactorial falls prevention. Otago-style exercise and home-hazard interventions reduce fall incidence by up to 32%.

citations.title: Multifactorial falls risk screening outperforms single-test approaches
citations.finding: Multifactorial assessment combining strength, balance and gait measures identified significantly more at-risk older adults than any single screening tool.

citations.title: Functional Status and Readmissions
citations.finding: Functionally impaired patients face up to 42% higher readmission risk.

citations.title: Functional Impairment and Post-Discharge Costs
citations.journal: Journal of the American Geriatrics Society
citations.finding: Functionally impaired patients face 77% higher post-discharge costs.

citations.title: Functional Decline in Medicare Patients
citations.finding: Functional decline affects over half of Medicare patients, significantly increasing healthcare utilization.

citations.title: Functional Independence Limitations and Healthcare Costs
citations.finding: Functional independence limitations cost US healthcare $24,000 per patient annually.

citations.title: Cost-effectiveness of a physical activity program on functional mobility decline in older adults
citations.finding: Prevention programs give a 0.04 QALY health benefit, demonstrating cost-effectiveness of functional health interventions.

blocks.heading: Talk to the clinical team about the evidence.
blocks.bodyText: Our clinical team can walk you through the research and how it applies to your setting.

blocks.heading: Grip Strength: The Essential Biomarker for Longevity
blocks.promoBody: Download the full clinical summary — peer-reviewed evidence linking grip strength to biological age, mortality, frailty and chronic disease risk, with guidance on integrating grip testing into routine assessment.
